name: CockroachDB
description: Distributed SQL database designed for high availability, effortless scale, and control over data placement

license.comment: Changed from BSL to proprietary CockroachDB Software License in November 2024. Older versions (21.1 and earlier) are Apache 2.0.

hosting-options.comment: CockroachDB Cloud offers Basic (serverless), Standard, and Advanced (dedicated) tiers. Also available via third-party providers.

initial-release.comment: Development started in 2014, first public release under Apache 2.0 in 2015 when Cockroach Labs was founded.

topology.comment: Uses Raft consensus protocol with per-range leader election. Each range has a leaseholder that coordinates reads and writes.

sharding.comment: Data is divided into ranges (default 64MB) that are automatically split, merged, and rebalanced. Range-based sharding on primary key.

replication-factor.comment: Default replication factor is 3. Configurable per zone/table.

multi-region.comment: Native support for global deployments with locality-aware data placement and geo-partitioning.

cap-position.comment: Prioritizes consistency over availability. May reject writes during network partitions to maintain consistency.

consistency-model.comment: Offers serializable isolation by default. Provides single-key linearizability and 'no stale reads' guarantee.

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID transaction support across distributed data, even for globally distributed workloads.

transaction-scope.comment: Transactions can span arbitrary tables and rows across the entire cluster.

isolation-levels.comment: Offers SERIALIZABLE (default) and READ COMMITTED isolation levels.

distributed-transactions.comment: Uses Raft consensus for distributed transaction coordination across nodes.

sql-compatibility.comment: PostgreSQL wire protocol compatible. Most PostgreSQL drivers work without modification.

joins.comment: Full distributed JOIN support across tables and partitions.

full-text-search.comment: PostgreSQL-compatible full-text search with TSVECTOR/TSQUERY since v23.1. Uses GIN indexes.

max-cluster-size.comment: Near-linear performance scaling demonstrated at 256 nodes.

write-throughput.comment: CockroachDB v21.1 achieved 1.68M tpmC with 140K warehouses on 81 c5d.9xlarge nodes.

read-latency-p99.comment: p99 ~79.7ms under TPC-C benchmark. Single-row reads ~1ms within single AZ.

auto-scaling.comment: Automatic rebalancing when nodes are added/removed. CockroachDB Cloud serverless tier auto-scales.

hot-spot-handling.comment: Automatic range splitting and rebalancing to handle hot spots.

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrades supported without service interruption.

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema changes run as background jobs without locking tables. Based on Google F1 design.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Supports incremental backups with revision history for point-in-time recovery. Subject to GC TTL.

backup-strategy.comment: Full and incremental backups to cloud storage. CDC changefeeds can be used for backup/replication.

kubernetes-operator.comment: Official Kubernetes operator maintained by Cockroach Labs.

rust-driver.comment: Uses rust-postgres driver via PostgreSQL wire protocol compatibility.

go-driver.comment: Official cockroach-go package with transaction retry helpers. Also supports pgx and pq drivers.

java-driver.comment: Uses PostgreSQL JDBC driver with official Hibernate dialect support.

dotnet-driver.comment: Uses Npgsql (PostgreSQL .NET driver) via wire protocol compatibility.

python-driver.comment: Supports psycopg2/psycopg3 with official sqlalchemy-cockroachdb adapter.

ruby-driver.comment: Uses pg gem with official ActiveRecord CockroachDB adapter maintained by Cockroach Labs.

c-cpp-driver.comment: Uses libpqxx (PostgreSQL C++ driver) via wire protocol compatibility.

orm-support.comment: Official support for Hibernate (Java), SQLAlchemy (Python), Prisma (TypeScript), GORM (Go), ActiveRecord (Ruby), and Django ORM.

open-source.comment: Changed to proprietary CockroachDB Software License in November 2024. Older versions remain under Apache 2.0 or BSL.

free-tier.comment: Basic (serverless) tier includes 10 GiB storage and 50M Request Units free per month.

pricing-model.comment: Pricing varies by tier: Basic uses per-request (RU), Standard/Advanced use per-vCPU and per-storage.

cost-estimate-small.comment: Varies by cloud provider and region. Basic tier free up to limits; production Standard starts around $200/month.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Unable to retrieve exact count from Stack Overflow.

change-data-capture.comment: Built-in changefeeds with support for Kafka, cloud storage, webhooks, and other sinks.
